data.title: CiDi Inc. Updates Articles of Association Following Hong Kong H-Share Listing
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CiDi Inc. Class H ( (HK:3881) ) has issued an announcement.

CiDi Inc. has amended its Articles of Association to reflect changes arising from the issuance and listing of its H shares on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on 19 December 2025. The revisions formalize the company’s updated share capital structure, including specifying 5,407,980 overseas listed foreign shares, setting registered capital at RMB 43,789,310 and total investment at RMB 76,762,660, and updating the total number of shares issued following its corporate restructuring. These adjustments, authorized by shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting in September 2024, align the company’s constitutional documents with regulatory requirements and its post-listing capital base, providing greater transparency and clarity for investors and regulators while leaving other provisions of the Articles unchanged.

More about CiDi Inc. Class H

CiDi Inc. is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China, transformed from Changsha Intelligent Driving Research Institute Company Limited as a whole. The company is listed on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange as an issuer of H shares, indicating a focus on the capital markets in Hong Kong to support its business development and growth.

Average Trading Volume: 115,389

Greentown Management Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:9979) ) has provided an update.

Greentown Management Holdings has called an extraordinary general meeting to be held on 2 February 2026 in Hangzhou, where shareholders will vote on the approval and adoption of a new 2025 Share Incentive Scheme. The proposed plan would authorize the board, or a designated committee, to grant awards, administer, and amend the scheme within the confines of Hong Kong listing rules, signaling a move to strengthen long-term incentives for management and employees and align their interests more closely with shareholders, while formal poll procedures, proxy arrangements and a temporary share register closure have been set to govern participation and voting.

Listed in Hong Kong, the company’s operations span exploration, drilling, production and processing of a range of crude types, with a particular emphasis on heavy oil and bitumen projects in Alberta, including thermal facilities such as West Ells.

EPI (Holdings) Limited has proposed a rights issue to raise up to approximately HK$198.2 million before expenses by offering two rights shares for every one existing share held by qualifying shareholders at a subscription price of HK$0.16 per rights share, which could result in the issuance of up to 1,238,487,808 new shares. China Everbright Bank Co ( (HK:6818) ) just unveiled an update.

China Everbright Bank has declared an interim ordinary cash dividend of RMB 1.05 per 10 shares for the six months ended 30 June 2025, payable to H-share holders in either Hong Kong dollars or renminbi, with a default payment of HKD 1.16634268 per 10 shares based on an exchange rate of RMB 1 to HKD 1.11080255. An announcement from RoboSense Technology Company., Ltd ( (HK:2498) ) is now available.

RoboSense Technology Co., Ltd. reported strong operational momentum in 2025, highlighting significant breakthroughs in new product launches and sales of its LiDAR product portfolio. For the fourth quarter of 2025, the group sold about 459,600 LiDAR units in total, including approximately 238,400 units for ADAS applications and 221,200 units for robotics and other uses, while full-year 2025 volumes reached about 912,000 LiDAR units overall, with 609,000 units for ADAS and 303,000 units for robotics and other segments. The unaudited figures suggest rapid scale-up and expanding market penetration across both automotive and robotics verticals, though the company cautioned investors that these preliminary operational data may differ from its forthcoming audited annual results and should be interpreted with care when assessing future growth and valuing its securities.

RoboSense Technology Co., Ltd. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated technology company focused on the development and sale of LiDAR products.
